results.rendered_html: dadhy—ac m. (nom. -aṅ acc. -añcam; dat. ˚dhīce gen. ˚dhīcas; cf.  Pāṇ. vi, 1, 170 ) ‘sprinkling dadhi’ (cf. ˚dhi-krā and ghṛtācI) N. of a mythical Ṛṣi or sacrificer ( RV. i, 80, 16  and [called Aṅgiras] 139, 9  [āṅgirasa]  TāṇḍyaBr. xii, 8 ; son of Atharvan [cf. ātharvaṇa]  RV. vi, 16, 14 ;  BhP. iv, 1, 42 ; having the head of a horse and teaching the Aśvins to find in Tvaṣṭṛ's house the madhu or Soma,  RV. i, 116 f.  and 119 ; ix, 108, 4 ; favoured by Indra [x, 48, 2 ] who slays 99 Vṛtras or foes with a thunderbolt made of his bones, i, 84, 13 ;  BhP. vi, 11, 20 ; viii, 20, 7 ; propounder of the Brāhmaṇa called madhu,  ŚBr. iv , xiv ;  BhP. vi, 9, 50 ff. )
